message:
fix(lowering): correct ternary expression lowering to use branch control flow instead of eager evaluation

Previously, conditional (ternary) expressions were lowered by eagerly
evaluating both the consequent and alternate branches and discarding the
consequent value with a Pop instruction. This was semantically incorrect:
it changed side-effect ordering and always left the alternate value on
the stack regardless of the condition.

The fix introduces proper branch control flow for ternary expressions:
- Emit JumpIfFalsy to skip the consequent when condition is false
- Emit Jump to skip the alternate when consequent was taken
- Use Label instructions for branch targets

This required threading `label_counter` through all
`lower_expression_to_ir1` call sites (new parameter added to the
function signature), ensuring globally unique label IDs across the
entire lowering pass.

Additionally fixes arrow function block bodies which previously created
an isolated `arrow_label_counter` starting at 0, which could produce
duplicate label IDs when the outer scope had already allocated labels.
Arrow blocks now share the outer `label_counter`.

Other changes in this commit:
- conformance_harness: simplify minimize_source_segments to use implicit
  return instead of intermediate binding
- Tests updated to validate new branch-based lowering:
  - lowering_coverage_integration: assert JumpIfFalsy presence and
    absence of Pop for conditionals
  - lowering_pipeline inline tests: verify two Labels, JumpIfFalsy,
    Jump, and no Pop for ternary lowering
  - New test: arrow_function_block_reuses_outer_label_counter ensures
    label uniqueness across nested arrow bodies
- Formatting/lint fixes across test suites:
  - BenchmarkSplit::as_str expectations updated to snake_case
  - Removed unused clone_id helper and unused imports (ExtensionManifest,
    SignedPackage, TransportEntry)
  - Added #[allow(dead_code)] and #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
    where appropriate
  - Rustfmt line-width adjustments in multiple test files
  - Fixed unused variable warning (_verdict)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
